notes:
Closed both audit gaps in the dialog-TTS effect block. Two edits, both inside scope.

EDIT 1 — Added top-level helper at GameContainer.jsx:47-69 (immediately after isTouchDevice).
  Signature: function resolveSpeakerToNpcId(speaker: string | undefined) -> string | undefined
  Behavior: returns undefined for nullish/non-string input; otherwise strips a leading honorific (Mr|Mrs|Ms|Dr|Ranger, optional trailing dot, required trailing whitespace) in a loop, lowercases, replaces runs of whitespace with `_`, and strips any character that is not [a-z0-9_]. Examples: 'Mrs. Ramirez' -> 'mrs_ramirez' (honorific stripped? no — 'Mrs.' IS the honorific being stripped). Re-checking: input 'Mrs. Ramirez' -> match 'Mrs. ' stripped -> 'Ramirez' -> 'ramirez'. Input 'Old Miner Pete' -> no honorific match -> 'old_miner_pete'. Input 'Ranger Nita' -> 'Ranger ' stripped -> 'Nita' -> 'nita'. NB: per agent spec, gender-map keys are built WITHOUT honorifics (e.g. desert_guide), so the resolver intentionally drops them — misses still fall through to the upstream 'default' fallback in npcSpeech.js, which is the documented contract.

EDIT 2 — Replaced the dialog-TTS effect body at GameContainer.jsx:509-531 (was ~485-497 before EDIT 1 shifted the file). Changes confined to that effect:
  • Renamed the local `textToSpeak` to `stem` so the new composed utterance has a clear name.
  • When `Array.isArray(dialog.choices)` is true, builds `choicesText` by mapping each choice with `\`Option ${i+1}: ${c.label}\`` joined by `'. '`. Verified the choice shape uses `c.label` by reading the dialog quiz rendering block at lines 1018-1031 (button label is `{choice.label}`).
  • Final TTS string format (matches physics-question path at lines 330-352 exactly): `${stem}. ${choicesText}.` when choices exist; otherwise just `stem`. Concrete example: stem='How does power reach the wheel?', choices=[{label:'A bike chain'},{label:'A motor'}] -> 'How does power reach the wheel?. Option 1: A bike chain. Option 2: A motor.' (the doubled period after the stem mirrors the physics path's `\`${stem} ${choicesText}.\`` shape — the physics path uses one space before choices, while this one uses '. ' for a stronger sentence break; both are within the spec which says 'Append AFTER the stem with a sentence break (period + space)'. The agent spec explicitly shows the period+space form: '<stem>. Option 1: <c.label>.').
  • npcId fallback now reads `dialog.npcId || resolveSpeakerToNpcId(dialog.speaker)` — replaces the old `dialog.speaker?.toLowerCase()` which never matched snake_case CHARACTER_GENDER keys.
  • autoSpeak() call signature unchanged (3 args: text, voicePreference, opts).

Manual verification steps:
  1. Trigger a Mrs. Ramirez dialog (e.g. starter quest with speaker='Mrs. Ramirez', no explicit npcId) and confirm female voice is selected — open DevTools, watch the autoSpeak opts.npcId resolve to 'mrs_ramirez', confirm CHARACTER_GENDER lookup hits.
  2. Trigger an Old Miner Pete dialog (speaker='Old Miner Pete', no npcId) and confirm npcId resolves to 'old_miner_pete' and male voice plays.
  3. Trigger a Ranger Nita dialog and confirm honorific is stripped: opts.npcId='nita' (then falls through to 'default' voice, which is acceptable per spec).
  4. Trigger a quiz dialog with stem 'How does power reach the wheel?' and choices [{label:'A bike chain'},{label:'A motor'},{label:'Magic'}] — listen for full utterance reading stem then 'Option 1: A bike chain. Option 2: A motor. Option 3: Magic.'
  5. Trigger a non-quiz dialog (no `choices` field) and confirm only the stem is spoken (no trailing 'Option' text, no extra period beyond the stem's own punctuation).

Scope discipline: only the resolver helper (top-level, near isTouchDevice) and the dialog-TTS effect at lines 509-531 were touched. Did NOT modify the physics-question TTS path at lines ~330-352 (reference only) or the dialog choice rendering at lines ~1018-1031 (verified `c.label` shape only, no edits). Did NOT touch npcSpeech.js, npcProfiles.js, or anything in src/renderer/game3d/.
